name: Captain Training
briefing: Welcome to Captain Training. Practice using the command dashboard — reviewing crew and mission status, managing alert levels, and writing log entries. The Captain coordinates all stations and makes final decisions.

objectives.text: Review ship status — check hull, shields, and all system efficiencies
objectives.hint: The Captain's dashboard shows a summary of every ship system. Look for any systems below 80% efficiency — these need Engineering attention. The tactical map shows contact positions. Brief your crew — what do you see?

objectives.text: Add a log entry about the current situation
objectives.hint: Use the LOG ENTRY field at the bottom of the panel. Write a brief status update (e.g. 'Contact bearing 045, distance 15k — raising alert level'). Log entries are timestamped and saved for the debrief. Press ADD or press Enter.

As Captain, your primary job is coordination. Observe your tactical map and crew status displays. In a real mission, you would be directing: 'Helm, set heading 045. Weapons, standby to fire. Science, scan that contact.' Practise giving clear, station-specific orders.

objectives.text: Add a final log entry to conclude the training drill
objectives.hint: Write a closing log entry (e.g. 'Drill complete — all stations performed well. Returning to standard alert.'). In a real game, your log entries help the crew review the mission in the debrief. Press ADD to complete.
